specific_mechanism: SVFT: Symplectic-Verlet Fine-Tuning. Replace momentum SGD with Stormer-Verlet leapfrog (kick-drift-kick) updates that preserve phase-space symplectic 2-form. Energy-conservation residual r_t = |H_t - H_0| is tracked; when r_t > ε, an energy-rebalancing projection rescales momentum to restore H_t ≈ H_0. This gives bounded long-term drift from the pre-training manifold.
llm_application: SVFT (Symplectic-Verlet Fine-Tuning): The optimizer state is (θ, p). Each step is a kick-drift-kick leapfrog: p_{t+1/2} = p_t - (η/2) ∇L(θ_t); θ_{t+1} = θ_t + η p_{t+1/2}/β; p_{t+1} = p_{t+1/2} - (η/2) ∇L(θ_{t+1}). After every K steps, compute H_t = L(θ_t) + ‖p_t‖²/(2β); if |H_t-H_0|/|H_0| > ε (default 0.05), project p_t to satisfy H_t = H_0 by rescaling. Time-reversibility holds; phase-space volume is preserved analytically.
novelty_claim: Stormer-Verlet leapfrog with explicit energy-conservation residual projection as a fine-tuning optimizer. Hamiltonian Descent (Maddison 2018) used implicit Euler not leapfrog; symplectic neural networks model physical systems, not LLM fine-tuning.
motivation_justification: Momentum SGD literally IS a discrete-time Hamiltonian integrator. The symplectic 2-form preservation is an exact algebraic identity that bounds energy drift. Mechanism transfer is direct, not analogical.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: Stormer-Verlet kick-drift-kick leapfrog momentum step
M_2: Time-reversible symplectic update preserving dq∧dp
M_4: Momentum-rescaling projection when r_t > ε
content_words: optimizer fine-tuning bounded drift pre-training manifold
momentum projection back to constant-energy shell

llm_side: optimizer fine-tuning bounded drift pre-training manifold
momentum projection back to constant-energy shell
